--- layout: default title: Hotkeys ---
masautt / hotkeys

Hotkeys

The desktop shortcuts I actually use on Ubuntu. They're GNOME custom keybindings, generated straight from media-keys.dconf in masautt-inc/config — the same file that configures the live machine, so this page can't drift from it. Every binding has a shell twin that runs the same engine.

Bindings
KeysWhat it doesRuns
Ctrl+Alt+T Plain Terminal (no Claude) ghostty --gtk-single-instance=true -e zsh -i
Ctrl+Alt+B Cycle animated background hanabi-bg next
Ctrl+Alt+Shift+B Cycle animated background (previous) hanabi-bg prev
Ctrl+Alt+O Opacity down (more wallpaper) ghostty-opacity down
Ctrl+Alt+Shift+O Opacity up (less wallpaper) ghostty-opacity up
Commands

Each shortcut above is a shell function too — same engine, same behaviour, tab-completion included.

bg wraps hanabi-bg

Switch the animated wallpaper. The live background is the Hanabi GNOME extension playing a looping video; this picks which one.

  bg                    # list the videos, marking the current one
  bg <name>|<n>         # switch (filename substring, or list number)
  bg next | prev        # cycle forward / backward
opacity wraps ghostty-opacity

Ghostty's background opacity. Lower means more of the wallpaper shows through. Changes hot-reload — the engine signals Ghostty, no restart.

  opacity               # print the current value
  opacity <0-1>         # set it
  opacity down | up     # step by 0.1
plain wraps ghostty

A plain Ghostty window: a normal interactive zsh with no Claude and no tmux stack. The default launcher starts Claude on top, so this is the escape hatch.

  plain                 # open in the current directory
  plain <dir>           # open in <dir>
